explicit_text: I was born in Hue, Vietnam, in 1996 and spent my early life there. My school life in Hue was great; I played basketball for my school team and had many friends. Leaving Hue to move to the United States in 2013 was a big change that made me lose the comfortable life I had there.
implicit_text: I was born in a historic city in central Vietnam in 1996 and spent my early life there. My school life in that city was great; I played basketball for my school team and had many friends. Leaving that city to move to the United States in 2013 was a big change that made me lose the comfortable life I had there.

explicit_text: Vietnam was my home until 2013 when my family and I moved to the United States. Life in Vietnam was good; I attended one of the top schools and had a supportive social circle. Leaving Vietnam meant leaving behind a familiar culture and language, which was very challenging when I arrived in the US.
implicit_text: My home country was where I lived until 2013 when my family and I moved to the United States. Life there was good; I attended one of the top schools and had a supportive social circle. Leaving that country meant leaving behind a familiar culture and language, which was very challenging when I arrived in the US.

explicit_text: My family and I moved to the United States about four years ago to start a new life. At first, I struggled a lot with English and fitting in at school, especially at Richfield High School. Over time, with the help of my job at Genesys Works and daily practice, I improved my English and became more independent as I prepared for graduation.
implicit_text: My family and I moved to North America about four years ago to start a new life. At first, I struggled a lot with English and fitting in at school, especially at the local high school in Richfield. Over time, with the help of my job at a nonprofit organization and daily practice, I improved my English and became more independent as I prepared for graduation.

explicit_text: One week after arriving in the US, I enrolled in 11th grade at Richfield High School to prepare for the ACT. It was difficult to blend in because of my limited English skills, and I disliked group work and discussions since I had to speak a lot. Despite these challenges, attending Richfield High was an important step in adjusting to life in the United States.
implicit_text: One week after arriving in the US, I enrolled in 11th grade at the public high school in Richfield to prepare for a college entrance exam. It was difficult to blend in because of my limited English skills, and I disliked group work and discussions since I had to speak a lot. Despite these challenges, attending that high school was an important step in adjusting to life in the United States.

explicit_text: I had to prepare for the ACT after moving to the United States, which was one reason I repeated 11th grade at Richfield High School. Preparing for the ACT was tough because of my limited English skills, but it motivated me to improve my language abilities. It was a key milestone in my academic journey here.
implicit_text: I had to prepare for a standardized college admissions test after moving to the United States, which was one reason I repeated 11th grade at the high school in Richfield. Preparing for this exam was tough because of my limited English skills, but it motivated me to improve my language abilities. It was a key milestone in my academic journey here.

explicit_text: Genesys Works came to my school to recruit students, and even though I was unsure, I applied and was accepted. Working there for a year helped me improve my English speaking and listening skills by interacting with coworkers daily. It also gave me my first opportunity to earn money in the US, which allowed me to buy shoes for my mom and save for trips back to Vietnam.
